title: Motion In One Dimension - Study Notes

sections.heading: Distance, Speed, Displacement, and Velocity
sections.content: Basic kinematic definitions and average value formulas.
formulas: Avg. speed = Total distance / Total time
Avg. velocity = Total displacement / Total time
If a body moves a certain distance with speed V_1 and returns to the same point with speed V_2, then Average speed <V> = (2 V_1 V_2) / (V_1 + V_2)
For equal distances with speeds V_1, V_2, ... V_n, the average speed is the Harmonic Mean: n / <V> = 1/V_1 + 1/V_2 + ... + 1/V_n
For equal time intervals with different speeds, the average speed is the Arithmetic Mean: <V> = (V_1 + V_2 + ... + V_n) / n
specialCases: Speed is always a positive quantity, however it may increase or decrease with time.
For uniform motion: Distance covered = magnitude of displacement; motion is along a straight line; direction does not change.

sections.heading: Kinematics and Acceleration
sections.content: Equations of kinematics are applicable for constant acceleration. The velocity and acceleration of a body need not be in the same direction or zero simultaneously.
formulas: If a body travels with uniform acceleration a_1 for t_1 and a_2 for t_2, Average acceleration = (a_1 t_1 + a_2 t_2) / (t_1 + t_2)
For uniform acceleration, Average velocity = (u + v) / 2
specialCases: Distance travelled in successive seconds is in the ratio 1:3:5:7...
When starting from rest, distances travelled in the first 1s, 2s, 3s... are in the ratio 1:4:9:16...
If distance s ∝ t, acceleration is zero.
If s ∝ t², acceleration is constant.
If s ∝ t³, acceleration varies as time (a ∝ t).

sections.heading: Graphical Analysis
sections.content: Analysis of x-t (position-time) and v-t (velocity-time) graphs.
properties: x-t graph parallel to time axis: body is at rest.
x-t graph straight line inclined to time axis: constant velocity.
x-t graph slope decreasing: velocity decreasing (retarded motion).
x-t graph slope increasing: velocity increasing (accelerated motion).
v-t graph parallel to time axis: acceleration is zero.
v-t graph straight line inclined with positive slope: constant acceleration.
v-t graph straight line inclined with negative slope: constant retardation.
Area between v-t graph and time axis gives displacement / change in position.
Area between a-t graph and time axis gives change in velocity.

sections.heading: Motion Under Gravity
sections.content: Kinematics applied to free fall and vertical projection.
formulas: Maximum height attained (thrown upwards): h = u² / (2g)
Time of rise (if air resistance is negligible) = Time of fall = u / g
Packet dropped from ascending balloon (velocity u, height h): Time to reach ground t = u/g + √((u/g)² + 2h/g)
If 'n' balls are thrown per second such that the next is thrown when the previous is at max height, maximum height h = g / (2n²)
specialCases: At highest point, effective g = g.
If air resistance is not neglected, time of descent > time of ascent.
Body returns to the starting point with the same velocity with which it was thrown.
A freely dropped body and a horizontally projected body from the same height reach the ground at the same time.

sections.heading: Relative Velocity and Applications
applications: Rain-man problem: Rain falling at v, man moving at u. Umbrella angle with vertical: tan θ = u / v
River-boat (shortest time): Boat moves at right angle to current. Time t = D / v
River-boat (shortest path): Boat moves at angle sin⁻¹(u/v) with vertical. Time t = D / √(v² - u²)
